Credit Rating.description: Your level of income and social class standing.

Jump.description: You can reduce falling damage by half with a successful use. Typical jump distance is roughly equal to the character's height.

Cthulhu Mythos.description: Represents knowledge of the cosmic horrors and forbidden truths. This skill is gained only through exposure to the supernatural or studying forbidden tomes. Contact with the Mythos always threatens Sanity.

Anthropology.description: The ability to study people through observation and analysis of behavior, customs, traditions, and culture.

Archaeology.description: Lets you date and identify traces of past cultures and distinguish fakes from different historical periods.

Accounting.description: Skill in handling money, financial records, and identifying irregularities in accounts.

Ride.description: Skill in riding horses, donkeys, camels, or similar animals, including basic care and handling.

Survival.description: Wilderness survival skills: hunting, shelter building, finding food and water, and recognizing dangerous plants and animals.

Track.description: The ability to follow people, vehicles, or animals and interpret the traces they leave behind.

Drive Auto.description: Skill in driving cars, motorcycles, or trucks, including road rules, basic repairs, and high-pressure chases.

Operate Heavy Machinery.description: Use of trains, bulldozers, cranes, excavators, tractors, and other heavy equipment.

Pilot.description: Training to operate aircraft safely, including takeoff, navigation, and landing under normal conditions.

Sleight of Hand.description: Allows concealing items on your person, pickpocketing, and quick manipulations without being noticed.

Intimidate.description: Use threats, presence, or fear to pressure others into compliance or silence.

History.description: Knowledge of historical events, timelines, notable figures, and the context of past eras.

Library Use.description: Ability to find books, newspapers, and archival records. Usually requires several hours of research.

Climb.description: Scale walls, trees, ropes, and difficult surfaces while managing fatigue and risk.

Disguise.description: Alter appearance, clothing, and mannerisms to pass as someone else or avoid recognition.

Medicine.description: Diagnosis and treatment of injuries and illnesses using professional medical knowledge.

Art/Craft.description: Create or evaluate works in a chosen art or craft specialty, from painting to carpentry.

Own Language.description: Reading, writing, and clear expression in your native language, including nuanced comprehension.

Foreign Language.description: Reading, writing, and speaking a non-native language with practical fluency.

Science.description: Understanding and application of scientific principles in a chosen field such as chemistry, biology, or physics.

Stealth.description: Move quietly, stay out of sight, and avoid drawing attention.

Fast Talk.description: Use quick lies, excuses, and verbal misdirection to confuse or mislead a target.

Occult.description: Identify cults by symbols, clothing, terminology, and rites, and recognize rituals, spells, and magical artifacts.

Navigate.description: Find your way in unfamiliar areas using maps, compass bearings, landmarks, or stars.

Appraise.description: Estimate the value, authenticity, and condition of property, antiques, or traded goods.

First Aid.description: Provide immediate treatment to stabilize injuries and prevent conditions from worsening.

Swim.description: Move effectively in water, stay afloat, and handle difficult currents or long distances.

Spot Hidden.description: Notice concealed objects, subtle clues, or details others might miss.

Law.description: Knowledge of legal systems, procedures, crimes, and rights relevant to investigations.

Natural World.description: Recognize plants, animals, weather patterns, and ecological signs in the environment.

Psychoanalysis.description: Once per month, can restore Sanity (1d3) or lose 1d6 on a failed attempt. A success may briefly suppress a phobia, mania, or hallucination.

Psychology.description: Form judgments about motives and personality traits and detect deception. The Keeper may roll secretly to mislead investigators.

Persuade.description: Convince others through reason, trust, and social influence rather than threats or trickery.

Repair (Electrical).description: Install, diagnose, and repair electrical circuits, wiring, and powered devices.

Repair (Mechanical).description: Maintain and fix mechanical systems such as engines, mechanisms, and tools.

Listen.description: Detect faint sounds, conversations, movement, or other important audio cues.

Locksmith.description: Open, bypass, or repair locks and simple security mechanisms.

Charm.description: Win people over through likability, warmth, and social grace.

Fighting (Brawl).description: Ability to fight in close combat with fists, knives, clubs, axes, swords, and similar melee weapons.

Throw.description: Any hand-sized object can be thrown at a target within range (up to extreme STR in meters), including knives, grenades, stones, and spears.

Firearms (Handgun).description: Skill in using handguns safely and effectively. Anyone can attempt a basic shot with a pistol.

Firearms (Rifle/Shotgun).description: Skill in using rifles, shotguns, carbines, and similar long firearms.

Firearms (Bow/Crossbow).description: Bows and crossbows require dedicated training and are treated as a separate weapon skill.
